summary.intent: Implement the framework-agnostic `layout::collapsible` component (issue #275) in `ars-components` with full test coverage, then reorganize layout snapshots into per-component directories to match the utility category pattern.
summary.outcome: The Collapsible core was implemented and shipped as PR #684 with 17 unit tests, spec-conformance, proptest, 9 snapshots, 94% line coverage, and 0 mutation survivors. Layout snapshot directories were restructured per-component. Two Codex review findings (controlled fallback state and keydown handled signal) were addressed in a follow-up commit.

repo: Layout modules should use directory-backed `<component>/mod.rs` layout (not flat `.rs` files) when they own snapshots, matching the utility category convention.
Snapshots belong in `crates/ars-components/src/layout/<component>/snapshots/` — a single shared `layout/snapshots/` directory is not the expected pattern.
Issue project-board status is updated via `gh project item-edit` with `--project-id`, `--id` (PVTI_...), `--field-id` (PVTSSF_...), and `--single-select-option-id`.
The Codex review workflow uses emoji reactions: 👀 = reviewing in progress, 👍 = approved; threads appear as unresolved GraphQL `reviewThreads` nodes.

`cargo xci-fast` is the required pre-push gate; mutation testing (`cargo mutants -f <file>`) and `cargo llvm-cov` are post-implementation audit gates that run before commit but after named tests are green.
The `post-implementation-audit` skill runs two 'anything else missing?' rounds and requires spec/adapter-spec drift to be fixed in the same changeset.
Adapter specs (Leptos, Dioxus) must be kept in sync with core event semantics — in particular, controlled `open` changes use `SyncControlledOpen`, not `SetOpen`.
The `cargo xtask spec validate` command validates all spec files and catches drift from the markdown contract.

code.finding: SyncProps was accidentally dropping controlled mode when collapsed-size props changed — prop-sync must preserve the controlled/uncontrolled distinction and only emit SyncControlledOpen when the controlled `open` prop itself changes.
The trigger keydown handler must return a handled/preventDefault signal so framework adapters can suppress the browser's native button click and avoid a double toggle.

When transitioning from controlled to uncontrolled mode, the last controlled `open` value must be preserved as the Bindable's internal fallback — otherwise the component snaps to the default instead of the last user-visible state.

workflow: `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` must be run against the full test surface, not a filtered subset, otherwise every snapshot not touched by the filtered run appears orphaned. CI is the correct place for this check.
When a flat Rust module is converted to a directory-backed `mod.rs` with identical code, a second mutation run against the new path is not necessary — the previous `0 missed` result is still valid.

Mutation test survivors fall into predictable categories: disabled guards, prop-sync branches, handler no-ops, and controlled/uncontrolled sync conditions. Targeted unit tests (not skip lists) are the right fix for each.

Snapshot file routing by name prefix can misroute if a component's test names include another component's name (e.g., `frame_*aspect_ratio.snap` matched the aspect_ratio bucket). Verify routing after bulk moves.
Running `cargo mutants` first with a broad file scope and then adding only the tests that kill specific survivors is more efficient than writing exhaustive tests upfront.

friction: `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject -- collapsible` fails whenever run with a test filter because it scans the whole snapshot tree but only references snapshots touched by the filtered tests — the error message looks like a Collapsible failure but is not.
Snapshot filename routing via shell `case` patterns during bulk `git mv` misassigned two frame snapshots (`frame_*aspect_ratio.snap`) to the aspect_ratio bucket, requiring a corrective move pass.
Mutation testing was mistakenly rerun after the file path changed from `collapsible.rs` to `collapsible/mod.rs` even though the code was identical — the user had to point this out.
The Codex review took an extended period (multiple 2-minute polling cycles, ~20+ minutes total) with no intermediate feedback visible before threads appeared.
The proptest uncovered a real controlled-mode bug (SyncProps dropping controlled state) that required a source fix mid-verification, adding an extra round of test/run cycles.
open_items: Full workspace `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` sweep to clear pre-existing orphaned snapshots in non-Collapsible layout components — currently deferred to CI.
Leptos and Dioxus adapter implementations for Collapsible are explicitly out of scope for issue #275 and will be separate tasks.
Pre-existing rustdoc link warnings in `ars-components` (not Collapsible-related) were observed during `cargo doc` but not addressed.
`cargo xci-fast` did not run corpus/snippet tests for the `collapsible` spec section because no matching test existed — spec snippet coverage for Collapsible is not yet wired up.
